commit.message:
doc: shed some light on ADT Object class anatomy

Because AI agents are always trying to access object metadata the wrong way.

adt: robustidize ADT's connection error handler

The handler checks if content is XML and if so it expects the common ADT
exception XML. The check use dict[] item access and if the header is not
present, it causes KeyError - this never happened in production but
AI agents generates tests data without it. To avoid confusion, do use
get() so there is no unexpected error.
http: explain exception handlers in comments

cli: support HUMAN format on dataelements

Basic support. Since I have no ADT documentation for the API, I need
more data elements to learn how to print out correctly.

It adds an extra feature - include domain details - that is meant for
better user experience to avoid the need to run additional query because
I believe the domain details are very important.

tree-wide: add support for domains

Again, modify operations are disabled because testing is trivial
and we do not have enough data.

I am happy to enable the operations, if somebody spends time testing it.

tree-wide: factor out ADT's http code into a shared module

This is the first step towards using shared code for HTTP communication
between all sapcli's modules. We need the shared module because we want
to enable authentication plugins providing SSO etc.

This patch touches also the module sap.rest a little bit because while
designing the new HTTPClient and was taking requirements of ADT, REST
and OData into account. It resulted into a little bit messy commit
but the future commit will be cleaner.

The HTTP communication tooling lives in sap/http now.

gh: fetch full git history when building the wheel

The `make dist` target computes the package version using
`git rev-list --count HEAD` to embed the total number of commits.
`actions/checkout@v4` defaults to a shallow clone (fetch-depth: 1),
which means only a single commit is fetched and the count is always 1.

Set `fetch-depth: 0` on the checkout step of the `publish-wheel` job so
the full history is available and the commit count reflects reality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tree-wide: add support for reading Authorization Fields

Basic support to dump authorization field data into command line.

The command line does not support create and delete because I had not
the nerves to test if it really works or not. If you are brave and have
more time, test it and enable it - do not for get to update the
automated tests.
doc: fix MD syntax
doc: display features before install
doc: add short windows install docu
gh: install build deps
gh: update trigger branch

cli: add the command find

This patch just exposes the ADT search functionality which already
exists in the code base as a new command.
doc: explain PACKAGE it gCTS commands

cosmetic: use ADT Object Type info in checkout

To make it it crystal clear where the code handling the object types is
located.

Not changed in the tests, there we do not want too much of dynamic
behavior. Also you can see the exercise with patch and copying OBJTYPE -
the patch replaced also OBJTYPE and that made the tests less valuable -
thanks to mistakenly pushed changed CI discovered the problem and I
decided to keep test using literals for object codes to get closer to
real ADT responses.

functionmodule: automatically find group name

All of the standard tools allow me to open function module
without passing function group. It looks like that function module names
have global namespace again. I only need to pass function group when
creating a new function module.

This patch introduces the possibility to pass '-' instead of function
group in function module commands. That keeps backward compatibility and
allows faster processing (if you leave function group out, sapcli must
do one extra HTTP round trip to find it).

config: add CRUD subcommands for connections, users, and contexts

Add 8 new CLI subcommands to manage the sapcli configuration file:
set-connection, delete-connection, get-connections, set-user,
delete-user, get-users, set-context, and delete-context.

Design decisions:

Naming follows kubectl/git conventions (set-*, delete-*, get-*).
The set-* commands use upsert semantics: they create the entry if
it does not exist, or merge the provided fields into the existing
entry if it does. This avoids the need for separate add/update
commands and keeps the CLI surface minimal.

Merge/patch behavior means that when updating an existing entry,
only the fields explicitly passed on the command line are changed;
all other fields are preserved. This is achieved by collecting
only non-None argument values and passing them to dict.update().
Boolean fields like --ssl need paired --ssl/--no-ssl flags with
default=None so we can distinguish 'not specified' from an explicit
True or False.

Referential integrity on delete: delete-connection and delete-user
refuse to proceed if any context references the named entry, unless
--force is passed. This prevents dangling references that would
cause confusing errors at runtime. delete-context has no such
constraint since contexts are leaf nodes in the reference graph.
Deleting the current-context automatically unsets current-context.

Each field has its own --flag rather than key=value pairs, which
provides better discoverability via --help, shell completion, and
type validation (e.g. --port is typed as int).

The --user flag in set-user uses dest='user_value' and in
set-context uses dest='user_ref' to avoid collisions with the
global --user argument that argparse injects into the namespace.
Similarly, set-context uses dest='ctx_password' for --password.

All new ConfigFile methods (_ensure_section, set_connection,
set_user, set_context, find_referencing_contexts, delete_connection,
delete_user, delete_context) are added to the model layer in
sap/config.py, keeping the CLI layer thin: commands just parse
arguments, delegate to ConfigFile, call save(), and print feedback.

Error handling uses SAPCliConfigError throughout, which the CLI
entry point already intercepts to print a user-friendly message
instead of a stack trace.

Also fixes a pre-existing mypy error by adding a type annotation
to the summary variable in merge_into().

fix: validate config sections in ConfigFile.load() at load time

ConfigFile.load() did not call _validate_config_data(), so malformed
section types (e.g. connections: []) were only caught lazily at
use-time by accessor methods.  Add the same validation call that
_fetch_config_from_path and _fetch_config_from_url already perform
so structural errors are rejected early with a clear message.

fix: validate context name and config existence in current-context command

The current-context command blindly printed any args.name without
checking it existed in the config, and did not check for a missing
config file (unlike use-context and view).

Now it returns non-zero with 'No configuration file found.' when
config is absent, and validates that a user-supplied context name
exists in config_file.contexts before printing it.

fix: apply config extra params even when args lacks the attributes

_apply_config_extra_params used hasattr() to guard setattr(), which
silently skipped config values when the attribute did not exist on
args—e.g. when using build_empty_connection_values() which omits
mshost, snc_*, etc.  Replace the hasattr+getattr guard with
getattr(args, param, None) so missing attributes are created by
setattr and populated from config_values.

fix: catch TypeError in config port parsing for non-string YAML values

int() raises TypeError for non-string/non-numeric values like None,
which YAML produces for bare 'port:' keys. Widen the except clause
from ValueError to (ValueError, TypeError) so malformed configs
consistently surface SAPCliConfigError.
doc: remove http_timeout from config file

Wire ssl_server_cert end-to-end through CLI, config, and all connection classes

ssl_server_cert was listed in CONNECTION_FIELDS and documented but
the config value was never propagated to any connection. The env var
SAP_SSL_SERVER_CERT only worked for ADT and REST (read directly via
os.environ in _get_session), not OData.

- Add --ssl-server-cert CLI argument in _entry.py
- Resolve ssl_server_cert in resolve_default_connection_values
  with precedence: CLI > SAP_SSL_SERVER_CERT env > config > None
- Accept ssl_server_cert=None in ADT, REST, and OData constructors
- Replace direct os.environ reads in ADT/REST _get_session with
  the constructor parameter; remove unused os imports
- Add ssl_server_cert support to OData (was completely missing)
- Pass ssl_server_cert through all connection factory functions
- Add ssl_server_cert to build_empty_connection_values
- Add 18 new tests across 5 test files

config: add merge command with --insecure and --skip-ssl-validation support

Add 'sapcli config merge --source <path-or-url>' command that merges
shared configuration files (local paths or HTTPS URLs) into the user's
personal config. Connections, users, and contexts are merged additively;
existing entries are preserved unless --overwrite is passed.

Support --insecure flag to allow plain HTTP source URLs and honor the
global --skip-ssl-validation flag to skip HTTPS certificate validation,
addressing environments with self-signed certs or missing CA bundles.

New functions in sap/config.py: merge_into(), fetch_config_source(),
_validate_config_data(), _fetch_config_from_url(), _fetch_config_from_path().

Includes 55 new tests covering validation, merge logic, URL/path fetching,
insecure/SSL wiring, and CLI integration.

configuration: initial version which mimics kube.config

I am not gonna lie to you, I just wrote basic details of how I picture
the configuration should work and then I asked Opus 4.6 to check how
other projects like kubectl solves it.

Eventually we decided to implement the most similar way to kubectl, so
users do not need to learn much.

The feature spec was like this:

sapcli is currently configured only via environment variables.
Users can easily set them but it is not very user-friendly.
Environment variables work fine in pipelines.

Considered other options:

- Add kubectl config style with connection (instead of server), user and
  context. We have many systems with equal configuration except the
  hostname - hence we a context specification must support overriding
  field from its referenced connection or user definition.

- Reuse SAPGUI `~/.SAPGUI/SAPGUILandscape.xml`: Could serve as a read-only source
  of connection definitions for desktop users. Does not contain credentials. Could be supported
  as an optional import/fallback, but should not be the primary config mechanism.

- Reuse ADT Eclipse workspace settings: Eclipse workspace settings are tightly coupled
  to the Eclipse workspace structure and would be fragile to parse. Deprioritized.

cli: detect invalid commands and print help

Not all execution paths have handler e.g. "sapcli" and without this
patch, the process dies with a stacktrace for missing member and that is
not a nice user experience.

gcts: filter user credentials by endpoint

For easy checks that user has valid credentials configured for the given
endpoint.

abap: print system information

Print system information retrieved from /sap/bc/adt/system/information
and /sap/bc/adt/core/http/systeminformation.

Renaming "systemID" to "SID" because that's what everybody would expect.

cli: recover from missing metadata

Doing packing correctly is so hard /o\

I need to find a better way for the fall back version
but I do not want to call git and rev-parse explicitly.

aunit: add the option to print missed statements

I find it very helpful when trying to improve coverage
or when asking agents to check if all their new/changed lines are
covered by tests.

The functionality is supposed to mimic Python coverage's flag '-m'.
fixup! adt: add common uri parsing helpers

adt: add the option to get statements position

A solo function because not everybody needs to parse the uri.
An explicit function to make the fact it can be parsed visible.
adt: add common uri parsing helpers

github: try to release new wheel upon push to master

At least some kind of release - unfortunately sapcli PiPY package is
taken.

make: add the dist target

Versions must follow:
https://peps.python.org/pep-0440/

I do not have capacity to carefully release version so I need an
autonomous rolling release with git commit hash included to make
sure can identify the repository state.

build: do not package build results

I sometimes build more times and then I start see "build/lib/build/lib".
build: correct dependencies

cli: use metadata version instead of the hack

Seems like the correct Pythonic way.
doc: simplify Ubuntu install with pipx
build: fix dependencies

gcts: add a convenience repo task list

The background task list is already covered but when dealing with
problems, I often do:

sapcli gcts repo activities <name>
sapcli gcts repo tasks <name>
sapcli gcts repo messages <name>

gcts: add push command

Surprisingly, gCTS push wants HTTP GET and returns nothing upon success
- except 200.

If error occurs, gCTS returns 4* or 5* and that is intercepted and a
nice error message is printed.

cli: print created files in checkout

To avoid the need to explain agents what was the checked out object
mapped to.

It is ok to use sap.cli.core.printout instead of
"args.console_factory()" because I do not plan to make this feature
available in any remote MCP.

tree-wide: enable running of custom ABAP snippets

Working on a feature for wrapping Function Modules execution
in a temporary class implementing if_oo_adt_classrun.

Running ABAP snippets from command line interface via the ADT Class
Execution.

The features should provide the same experience as passing script to
bash.

Use story - ABAP script file:
- a user needs to run 3 ABAP statements to do some trivial configuration
  which is not exposed by any remote functionality
- user writes those 3 statements into a file my_abap_script.abap
- user runs "sapcli abap run my_abap_script.abap"

Use story - generate ABAP script:
- a user needs to run an ABAP statement
- the statement contains a dynamically generated value - ENV with AWS key
- the user issues "echo 'cl_aws_lambda=>spanw( function = 'bar' key = '${AWS_KEY}'" | sapcli abap run -

How it works:
- sapcli creates a temporary class
- sapcli writes the class with implementation of the interface if_oo_adt_classrun
- sapcli activates the class
- sapcli executes the new class
- uncodontionally, sapcli deletes the class

Requirements:
- the new class description clearly explains the purpose so it can be deleted by anybody how discovers it
- the class is created in the package '$tmp' by default
- user have the option to change the package
- the class name follows the pattern "<prefix>_<username>_<random string>"
- the default <prefix> is zcl_sapcli_run
- the <username> is lowercased
- the user can provide their own prefix
- the class name must have 30 characters

Sample class:
```abap
"! This is a temporary class created by sapcli for execution
"! of an adhoc ABAP statements.
CLASS zcl_sapcli_developer_lfdjaslf DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_sapcli_developer_lfdjaslf IMPLEMENTATION.
    METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

        <user code>

    ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
```

Implementation:
- sap/adt/objects.py : implementation of ABAP Class operations (the class Class)
- sap/paltform/abap/run.py : logic for assembling the class code and execution of creation, execution, deletion
- sap/cli/abap.py : user interface

platform: support new TABLES Function Parameter spec

I am not exactly sure why but the parameter TABLES
does not allow STRUCTURE on my current ABAP runtime
and if I change to "TYPE" then I abapGit writes the RSTBL
column TYP instead of DBSTRUCT.

However, ADT format has not changed - it looks like that it always
supported `param_name TYPE (table | structure)`.

Since I have no time to test the behaviour on older ABAP systems,
I will just pray that this change where we change 'STRUCTURE'
to 'TYPE' even when exporting ADT format to abapGit format will not
cause too many problems.

I bet if ADT has TYPE with table, then abapGit format with "TYPE table"
will not be pullable into any system. To solve the issue, we would have
check the real type of the type spec used in the ADT format and I am not
sure it is worth my time now.

cli: expose whereused on all supported ADT objects

Very basic support just listing object type and object name.

I need the whereused functionality for recursive deletion of package and
I added whereused to command line interface just to show it is
supported.

adt: add initial support for Where Used queries

No special design, just exposure of types and methods
for searching ABAP objects - there are unsupported features
like version=active and selection.

I did not even test how to search for local objects and how does
the response with local object looks. All of that needs lot of sample
data and I have no time for that.
doc: make it clear RFC is not a must
doc: update the features section a little bit

connection: catch ReadTimeout and show SAPCLI_HTTP_TIMEOUT hint

Long running HTTP requests (especially synchronous gCTS operations)
were timing out with an uncaught requests.exceptions.ReadTimeout,
causing ugly stack traces instead of a helpful error message.

Catch ReadTimeout alongside the already handled ConnectTimeout in all
three connection modules (rest, adt, odata) and wrap it in the
sapcli-specific TimedOutRequestError.

The SAPCLI_HTTP_TIMEOUT hint is printed in the CLI entry point rather
than in the library-level exception message, keeping the error classes
presentation-agnostic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

adt: parse deletion responses

Very useful because objects might not be deletable for various reasons:
- package is not empty
- objects is used by another object

I am slowly discovering the use cases for deleting objects (not very
common activity for ABAPers).

package: fix recursive deletion

I missed that during review - the coding agent did fix the tests the
wrong way and it is not that easy to find a real system where you can
recursively delete an entire package. Hopefully I tested my new feature
on myself soon enough.
ddic: handle new FUGR metadata serialized by abapGit

checkin: parse zabapgit.prog.xml with CUA inside

I do not know how to push the CUA content to the target ABAP system
yet.

abapgit: support optional XML contents

Up until now, parsing of XML files generated by abapGit strictly
required the defined tags to catch bugs and be able to discover new data
abapGit puts into XML files and adapt sapcli to it (I cannot implement
the parser accordingly to some spec because  abapGit nor ABAP has a formal
definition - maybe AFL fill fix that).

tree-wide: support mass activation of objects of package

After a failed checkin execution, one needs to re-activate entire
package. The new command just helps me to do so.

cli: add recursive deletion of ABAP package

In the case I screwe up isntallation of abapGit I need to delete the
entire package $abapgit and start from scratch.

cli: enhance the object command template with delete

The method delete is already present, I was just lazy to add tests. The
situation is completely different with AI coding.

adt: factor out delete methods into utility functions

Because of my bad past decisions and because we do not want to create
instance of an objects just be able to create it (thinking about
"recursive deletion of a package where iteration over package
returns object ADT path (the uri property only)").

cmd: standardize checkin command definition

I ran into the same problem as with the command startrfc - using
non-standard ArgumentParser initialization leads into problems when
I define a new exotic default option such as "console_factory".
abap(git): add currently missing ABAP fields

aunit: port aunit to ADT API AUnit

The old behaviour is still available under the flag --compat

chore: more detailed ADT debug log

The goal always was to have everything captured in the tests but these
days I need to quickly see what exactly the tool sends.
doc: add examples for ADT annotations

tree-wide: prepare for packaging in pypi

- Add sap/cli/_entry.py with the full CLI entry point logic
- Reduce bin/sapcli to a thin shim delegating to sap.cli._entry.main
- Replace setup.py with pyproject.toml (modernise packaging)
- Add get_version.sh to derive version from git tags or pyproject.toml
- Add Makefile release targets: release-major, release-minor, release-fix
- Add RELEASING.md documenting the full release process
- Port tests from test_bin_sapcli.py to test_sap_cli__entry.py:
  - Drop importlib hack; import sap.cli._entry directly
  - Fix duplicate test_args_default_no_ssl → restore as test_args_default_sysnr
  - Patch builtins.input instead of sapcli.input
  - Patch sap.cli._entry.log.error for the KeyboardInterrupt assertion
- Delete obsolete test_bin_sapcli.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
doc: some simple docu for agents

cmd: fix bug in startrfc

Uh, I did not know I used to create own ArgParser outside sap.cli.core.

The patched file definitely deserves refactoring.

I am not even sure how to test this properly. The test uses generate_parse_args()
which sets the default argument console_factory. Hmm, I need to add
tests for all commands without the hacked args by tests.

tree-wide: add support for Behavior Definition

Generated by OpenCode based on captured ADT HTTP communication and my
previous commits.

I am little bit surprised I cannot see tests for the member
"abapsource:syntaxConfiguration" when creating the BDEF object.

tree-wide: add support for DCL

I followed the template of DDL but I am not sure I like it.
I will probably refactor to put DDL (CDS view) and DCL to (Acess
control) to shared module.

tree-wide: add long format to list of packages

To be able to easily display object type and description for
sub-packages I had to extend the function walk_step to return different
object when withdescr=true. I could change the returned object for both
cases but I wanted to keep the code backward compatible as much as
possible. I thought that currently nobody is calling walk_step with
withdescr=true.

adt: return object description in Package list

I am surprised the information was not returned before.
I can clearly see the description is fetched but not propagated.

test: fix context manager based mock checks

And other small issues caused by differences in Python libraries.

Revert "test: refactor CLI tests to improve module loading and error handling"

This reverts commit d439d4c95a4c9ef82c209c9b28aa74aa81bfb581.

I did terrible review. The reverted patch caused absolute mayhem.
The next patch will fix the tests correctly.

cli: do not use global Console by commands

I have just realized that my MCP server that runs the command functions
from HTTP handlers may suffer from race-conditions if the PrintConsole
objects is passed via the global variable. The problem was unlikely to
occur but if it can happen, it will happen.

To avoid too much of rewriting, I have decided to pass the PrintConsole
object as a new argument named console_factory. That keeps the highest
possible backward compatibility.

cts: enable transport type selection

Feature toggles requires customizing transports (type W) but the class
sap.adt.WorkbenchTransport had hardcoded type K. The K mean 'workbench'
and matches the class name. Perhaps I though it would be a good idea to
create different class for different Transport types but right now I
don't see it as good option.

I am tempted to renema WorkbenchTransport to something else because it
is no longer just a workbench transport, but it would definitely break
some existing Ansible modules.

tree-wide: basic support for Feature Toggles V2

No special object for FeatureToggle because It would be overkill and I
think I want to start adding support for new objects this way - data +
service manager (similar to Kubernetes libraries).

tests: enable changing content-type in JSON mocks

Because some new ADT endpoints accepts ADT enriched JSON Content-types.
tests: add the common parameters client and user to mocks

cli: port DDL/CDS to Object handler

To simplify and avoid the need to maintain several implementations.
And also automatically Upper case names because that is what ADT server
wants.

cli: all objects print via console

This was not problem until I build the MCP server which returns captured
console as its response.

strust: ignore S messages from th FM SSFR_PUT_CERTIFICATE

The new version returns information:
- Certificate added to PSE (SSFR_PUT_CERTIFICATE:204:SSLC/DFAULT)

The exact signature of the message is: TYPE=S ID=TRUST NO=003

We do ignore S message in other parts of the code, so lets do that also
with SSFR_PUT_CERTIFICATE.

gcts: safer handling of HistoryEntity rc

Since gCTS uses ABAP and ABAP often leaves out initial values and '0000'
is considered initial, we shall not die on missing 'rc' in
HistoryEntities.

After an ugly Ansible log "Module error: Task error: 'rc'", this patch
also updates exceptions messages.
gcts: recover from missing Client Response log

rfc: correctly parse RFC connection errors

The RFC library returns strangely formatted errors and the current parse
incorrectly expects each ERROR line starts with "ERROR". The incorrect
assumption leads to even more strange messages reported by sapcli.

This commits corrects the parser and lays down foundation for future
improvements.

Frankly speaking I am still not sure how the RFC library generates
splits the ERROR lines - mainly when and how to add white space.

If you are reading this because of the whitespaces, fix it please.

rfc: easier to read Exception error messages

I bet the standard is to write everything in lower case or something
like that but I am the one dealing with the most user reports containg
sapcli logs and this format helps me.

Also it helps me if I do not see duplicated information (the remove word
"error").

gcts: dump JSON messages from gCTS exception in __repr__

For debugging purposes and I pray it does not break anything.

gcts: detect another repo Already exists message

I still do not want to use regular expression because relying on
messages is scary enough and I afraid that a smart logic could cause
more troubles (e.g. catching more cases than expected).

gcts: predictable messages API types

I was annoyed by the fact that the method RemoteRepo.messages() returns
2 different types based on the parameter processes. However I did not
want to create two different methods (actions + action_processes).

This implementation keeps doors open for cool things like expanding the
action messages (e.g. download actions and also add processes).
doc: fix language in README

gcts: parse action messages for readable output

The goal is to avoid any meaningless technical data being displayed in
the default output.

I have done trivial analysis of several problems and selected these
sections of the process JSON object:
- Client : Client Response Log : Log (because the other ones are super
  technical and super redundant)
- Transport Tools : stdout (the reset makes no sense for mere mortals)
- gCTS (each item is a short message - so no brainer)

tree-wide: basic gcts repo log messages

This is initial version of gCTS log messages support.

I did not try to parse the messages into a python objects because the
structure is not super clear to me and I am not in the mood to try to
generalize their format.

If you see the need to have a formal format of the messages, please feel
free to define the model in sap/rest/gcts/log_messages.py
doc: add a note about running a single failed test

cli: add line callback for TableWriter

To be able to print something after the real line was printed out.

gcts: detect existing report repositories by another user

For the time being, we expect repositories get names generated from the
repository URL and thus if a repository of a certain name exists, we
expect it is the same ABAP code and thus it does not matter who created
the repo.

I have now realized that we should check that the repository which
already exists have the same URL as the one we wanted to create. This
check is important for automations where repo name can be force and not
generated from the URL (e.g. "ci-repo").
cli-strust: make documentation of -i/-s clearer
cli-ddls: add some trivial user help

cli: add description/help to Commands

Since we have been working on the MCP server which automatically exposes
commands, we need to provide some context to the tools in the form of
description which can be taken from help.

I didn't use the "help" because that's python build and thus this patch
introduces "description" which is closer to MCP.

I came to the conclusion that "command" description should be taken from
function handler "documentation" string because that looked naturally to
me - the handler should not be complicated. However I am not against
enhancing "CommandGroup.command" with the optional argument description.

cli: use virtual console for package list

Just for the MCP server.

cli: add function changing default console

Currently, for the MCP server but I am surprised I haven't needed it
yet.
